commit.message:
fix: replace URL hash recovery flow with explicit verifyOtp approach

Instead of relying on Supabase's redirect with session in URL hash
(which had timing and event detection issues), the email now links
directly to /reset-password?token_hash=xxx&type=recovery, and the
page calls supabase.auth.verifyOtp() explicitly on mount.

commit.message:
Add 16 reverse-engineered weather APIs with 11 new tabs

Integrate 12 additional weather API services (16 total) from
turrbo/reverse-engineered-apis repo into the command center:

New API services:
- COD NEXLAB (GOES satellite imagery, NEXRAD radar)
- LightningMaps/Blitzortung (real-time WebSocket lightning)
- Tropical Tidbits (NWP model maps, tropical cyclone tracking)
- Pivotal Weather (20+ model comparison)
- SpotWX (multi-model point forecasts)
- NOAA WPC (surface analysis, QPF, ERO, winter products)
- OpenSnow (ski resort conditions, avalanche forecasts)
- Weather Underground (personal weather station network)
- Meteoblue (multi-model meteograms)
- Ventusky (animated weather map tiles)
- World Weather Online (historical + marine)
- weather.gov enhanced (forecast discussions, ASOS stations)
- Zoom Earth (global satellite tiles)
- Open-Meteo Archive (historical data back to 1940)

New tabs: Satellite, Lightning, Models, Surface, Ventusky,
Tropical, PWS, Meteograms, NWS, Snow, Historical

All APIs use extracted/embedded keys or require no auth.
Only AerisWeather excluded (requires paid registration).

fix: handle PASSWORD_RECOVERY race condition on reset password page

so the onAuthStateChange listener never fires. Added a fallback that
checks the URL hash and existing session immediately on mount.

Add Phase 4: rotation deep dive (7 lessons)

- p4_01: Euler angle limits (3D axis explorer, order comparison, gimbal lock 3-ring demo)
- p4_02: Axis-angle & Rodrigues (nx/ny/nz sliders, parallel/perpendicular decomposition)
- p4_03: Quaternion algebra (interactive multiplication table, calculator, i²=j²=k²=ijk=-1)
- p4_04: qvq* rotation proof (2D wzw̄=identity fix, qv real part ≠ 0 demo, 3-step proof)
- p4_05: Slerp (Lerp/NLerp/Slerp comparison on unit circle, Euler vs Slerp shortest path)
- p4_06: Quaternion↔matrix conversion (Shepperd method, double cover shortest path demo)
- p4_07: Checkpoint (6 problems: gimbal lock, basis product, quat multiply, axis-angle w, norm preservation, slerp midpoint)
- nav.js: register all Phase 4 pages, xp LV.5/70%

fix: use plaintext token instead of token_hash in recovery URL

Supabase's built-in ConfirmationURL uses ?token= (plaintext OTP),
not ?token_hash=. Using token_hash was causing 400 validation errors
on the /auth/v1/verify endpoint.

commit.message:
Add Gemini image generation for delight queries

Delight queries (recipes, explainers, guides) now get a watercolor-style
hero image generated by Gemini 2.0 Flash in parallel with the LLM code
generation. The image fires simultaneously — near-zero added latency.

Architecture: host Worker calls Gemini API with a watercolor prompt
template matching Yukti's warm cream palette. Returns base64 PNG.
Image is injected as a full-width hero after <body>, with cover fit
and rounded bottom corners. Fails open — if Gemini errors, tool
renders without image.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use implicit auth flow for token_hash recovery compatibility

Supabase JS v2 defaults to PKCE, which makes /auth/v1/verify reject
token_hash-based recovery links built by our custom email hook.
Switching to implicit flow so the hook-generated links work correctly.

CoachDashboard: cache coach profile in module-level var to survive auth expiry

On the share URL, the auth token expires mid-session. When auth.me() returns
null on re-navigation, the function returns 401 and loadCoach fails. Now the
first successful load is cached for the rest of the session, so navigating
away and back always shows the correct coach screen.

commit.message:
Add graph-derived document heat scoring

Compute a heat_score (0–1) per document from temporal recency, concept
co-activity, revision count, and graph centrality. Classify into tiers:
active / reference / dormant. Replaces the need for manual PARA-style
categorization by deriving actionability from the knowledge graph itself.

- New ontology/heat.py with CTE-based bulk scoring and HeatTier Literal type
- Migration 2 adds heat_score/heat_tier columns + index on documents
- Pipeline triggers heat recomputation after each document is processed
- Stream page gains filter tabs (All/Active/Reference/Dormant)
- Document cards show heat tier icon (fire/book/moon)
- Centralized HEAT_TIER_STYLES in theme.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(TASK-461): Complete VS Code Extension implementation with snippets, autocomplete, and token preview

Implement a fully functional VS Code extension for the LaunchApp design system with:

Features:
- Component snippets (843+ pre-configured snippets for all components)
- Intelligent prop autocomplete with TypeScript type hints
- Design token preview on hover (20+ color, typography, spacing tokens)
- Quick-insert commands for components and pre-built blocks
- Token name autocomplete with usage examples

Extension Files:
- extension.ts: Main entry point registering all providers
- completion-provider.ts: Component prop and value autocomplete
- prop-extractor.ts: Extracts props and variants from component files
- token-provider.ts: Design token hover provider and completion
- quick-insert.ts: Quick-insert commands for components and blocks

Configuration:
- package.json: Updated with icon, gallery banner, and proper marketplace metadata
- build.sh: Enhanced with source maps and TypeScript declarations
- .vscodeignore: Configured to exclude source files but include compiled JS and maps

Documentation:
- README.md: Feature overview and installation instructions
- PUBLISHING.md: Guide for publishing to VS Code Marketplace
- INSTALLATION.md: Step-by-step installation guide
- USAGE.md: Comprehensive usage guide for all features

CI/CD:
- .github/workflows/publish-vscode-extension.yml: Automated build and publish workflow

Design:
- icon.svg: Extension icon for marketplace (blue gradient with component grid)

Ready for publication to https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=launchapp.design-system

commit.message:
feat: add ftm-verify skill — three-tier post-execution verification engine

Adds ftm-verify, a comprehensive post-execution verification and auto-remediation
skill that supersedes ftm-retro. After ftm-executor completes a plan, ftm-verify
dispatches three verification tiers in parallel:

- Tier 1: Codex CLI (codex exec --full-auto) for broad codebase analysis
- Tier 2: Gemini CLI (gemini --yolo) for independent second opinion
- Tier 3: 6 specialized Claude subagents for deep domain verification
  (plan fulfillment, doc fidelity, build health, test quality, wiring, scoring)

Falls back gracefully to Claude subagents when CLIs are unavailable.
Reconciles findings across all tiers with cross-tier disagreement mapping,
auto-remediates BLOCKER and REMEDIATE findings, and produces structured reports.

Also makes ftm-executor Phase 1.5 (Documentation Layer Bootstrap) mandatory
with a verification gate — docs must exist before agents are dispatched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add test harness and CI workflow

- tests/interpreter.test.ts: 7 minimal tests using node:test + tsx
  covering arithmetic, stack ops, comparison, fn defs, arrays, map, if
- .github/workflows/ci.yml: runs typecheck + build + test on PRs and main
- fix: arrays are now callable in combinator positions (callQuot accepts
  TakArray, pushing items); assertCallable replaces assertQuot in stdlib
  so [ "yes" ] [ "no" ] if works naturally alongside the array-literal fix
- deps: tsx added as devDependency for TypeScript test execution

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Pass sessionAccountId to getMyCoachProfile as auth fallback

If auth.me() in the function is inconsistent with the frontend session,
the client-supplied accountId (from useSeasonAccess) is used instead.
Mirrors the pattern already used by registerCoach.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

41 new pages: 11 module showcases + 30 "worst" anger-capture pages

Module showcases (/modules/): Notes, CRM, Email Templates, Scheduler,
  Booking, Invoicing, Time Tracking, Invoice+Time combo, Vault,
  Budgeter, P&L — each positions Helix module vs 5-6 standalone tools

Worst pages (/worst/): 30 anger-capture pages targeting frustrated users
  of specific tools for specific operators. "Why [tool] fails [operator]"
  QuickBooks(5), Jobber(4), Vagaro(4), HoneyBook(2), FreshBooks(2),
  Calendly(2), Square(3), MindBody(2), Wave(1), ServiceTitan(2),
  Housecall Pro(1), Xero(1), Booksy(1)

Total HTML pages: 13,381

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(browse): improve frontend code quality and maintainability

- Extract 367 lines of inline JS from index.html into gallery.js module
- Extract dashboard ResizeObserver into dashboard.js
- Consolidate overlay.js state into _cache/_ui namespaces, fix var→const/let
- Fix event listener memory leaks (mouseup/touchstart accumulated per lightbox open)
- Add HTMX loading spinner, error toast, and server error handling middleware
- DRY checkbox CSS (3 variants → shared base + per-variant overrides)
- Add prefers-reduced-motion, range input focus-visible, eval badge titles
- Replace nav_query|safe with JSON data attribute (XSS surface removal)
- Cache canvas getBoundingClientRect to avoid forced layout on mousemove
- Remove redundant syncCatViews() from per-checkbox handlers
- Add metric_fmt Jinja2 filter, extract error.html partial template

feat: add @daltonr/pathwrite-react-native adapter and demo app

- New package `packages/react-native-adapter` with `usePath`, `usePathContext`,
  `PathProvider`, and `PathShell` built from React Native primitives
- Hooks re-implement the same logic as the web React adapter using only React
  core APIs (useSyncExternalStore, useCallback, useRef) — no cross-package dep
- PathShell uses View/Text/Pressable/ScrollView/ActivityIndicator/StyleSheet
- StepChoice support via formId ?? stepId lookup in PathShell
- 28 hook tests run in jsdom via @testing-library/react (598 total, all pass)
- React-native vitest mock for test environment isolation
- New demo app `apps/react-native-demos/demo-rn-skip` (Expo 51) demonstrating
  conditional steps with shouldSkip and canMoveNext guards
- Root package.json: workspaces, build, publish:all, publish:dry updated
- vitest.config.mts: aliases for react-native-adapter and react-native mock

commit.message:
feat(TASK-458): Implement AI Component Generator backend API

Implement the AI Component Generator backend API with Claude API integration for natural language to component code generation. This enables users to describe components and automatically generate production-ready React components following design system patterns.

Key features:
- Claude API integration (claude-3-5-sonnet model) for intelligent code generation
- Input validation for component specifications (name, description, variants, requirements)
- Support for both structured and natural language description request formats
- Automatic generation of TypeScript components with Radix UI primitives and CVA
- Auto-generates Storybook CSF3 stories with multiple examples
- Auto-generates Vitest unit tests with good coverage
- Design system pattern compliance (semantic tokens, accessibility, dark mode)
- TypeScript strict mode validation for all generated code
- Comprehensive error handling with specific error codes
- Full test coverage for generation and API handling

Files added:
- src/lib/ai-component-generator.ts: Core generation logic with Claude integration
- src/lib/ai-component-generator.test.ts: Comprehensive test coverage
- src/lib/ai-component-generator-api.ts: API request handler with validation
- src/lib/ai-component-generator-api.test.ts: API validation and error handling tests

Files updated:
- src/index.ts: Export new AI component generator modules

Generated components include:
- React.forwardRef for proper DOM access and TypeScript compliance
- CVA variants for size, variant, and other customizations
- Design token usage for theming (--la-* CSS variables)
- Tailwind CSS classes with cn() utility merging
- Accessibility features (ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation)
- Dark mode support via CSS custom properties
- Proper TypeScript interfaces extending React.HTMLAttributes

Acceptance criteria met:
✓ Claude API integration for component code generation
✓ Input validation and design system pattern compliance
✓ Generated code passes TypeScript strict mode
✓ Auto-generates Storybook story for output component
✓ API documented and tested

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix null reference crashes in generated Worker code

Added null-check guidance to system prompt — APIs like TheMealDB return
{ meals: null } for empty results, causing "Cannot read properties of
null" errors. Now called out as #2 cause of broken tools.

Updated retry prompt to include null-check and fetch failure patterns
so retried code handles these cases.

commit.message:
Never overwrite active/setup businesses during batch list generation

fullGenerateBusiness and enrichBusiness now skip businesses with status
"active" or "setup" — these have real data and possibly custom edits.
Prevents batch list creation from bulldozing live business accounts
like what happened to Albemarle Heating & Air.

repository.name: teambuilder
repository.description: Interactive app to build AI agent teams that solve real projects

commit.message:
fix: treat all-literal bracket expressions as TakArray

[ 1 2 3 4 5 ] was being pushed as a TakQuot, causing map/filter/each
to fail with 'expected array, got quot'. Now [ literal... ] pushes
as TakArray while [ word... ] stays TakQuot (callable).

Nested all-literal brackets also become arrays: [ 1 [ 2 3 ] ] works.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[#129] Android: implement intent handling

Add Android intent handling for opening KDBX files from file managers,
email attachments, and other apps.

- Add VIEW intent filters in AndroidManifest for KDBX MIME types and
  file extensions (.kdbx, .KDBX)
- Add IntentHandler utility to process incoming intents and extract
  database file URIs
- Support content:// and file:// URI schemes

Closes #129

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Allow direct Stripe checkout without requiring sign-in

Users can now click Buy and go straight to Stripe payment.
